event_name: Polymarket frontend vendor compromise and malicious browser code
title: Polymarket frontend vendor compromise and malicious browser code
confidence_reason: Direct vendor/research reporting supports the core incident; public technical completeness varies.

credentials_at_risk: browser wallet approvals, sessions, and transaction-signing context

artifact_analysis.execution_trigger: See analysis.md; exact trigger is unknown where not publicly disclosed.
payload_behavior: Polymarket said a compromise at a third-party vendor allowed malicious code to be injected into its website for some users, that it contained the incident, and that it was contacting and fully refunding affected users. TechCrunch independently obtained confirmation from a Polymarket spokesperson that user funds were stolen. PeckShield reported a related phishing campaign and approximately $3 million in losses; the exact technical injection path, vendor, code artifact, exposure window, and victim count remained publicly unclear.

browser_exposure.closure_condition: All served deployment IDs scoped, malicious assets removed and caches invalidated, affected sessions/wallet activity reviewed.

open_questions: Exact artifact/deployment identity and complete exposure window remain unknown.

defender_takeaways.detection: Scan exported evidence with the tested incident-specific hunt.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Correlate selector matches with deployment and identity timelines.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Disable the affected path, rotate exposed identities, and recover from verified artifacts.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Require scoped credentials, immutable deployment evidence, and independent release validation.

containment_complete: Affected delivery path disabled and exposed identities revoked
recovery_complete: Verified clean deployment and monitoring restored
closure_required: UTC timeline, evidence owner, complete inventory, downstream audit, and rotation evidence recorded
